methods.description: Preparation, counting, and identification of pollen grains from sediment cores, including sample treatment, spiking for influx calculation, microscopic counting, identification using reference materials, application of counting criteria, and exclusion of specific taxa for analysis.

steps.description: Preparation of pollen samples (n = 508; volume = 0.5 cm 3 ) followed standard protocols, including treatment with 10% HCl,10% KOH, 10% Na 4 P 2 O 7 , acetolysis, and sodium metatungstate flotation at a density between 2.0-2.1 g/mL 69 .
steps.searchable_summary: Chemically process sediment samples to isolate pollen using standard protocols including acid/base treatment, acetolysis, and flotation.

steps.description: Samples were spiked with a known quantity (~5000) of 15 µm polystyrene microspheres, to facilitate calculation of influx (grains per cm 2 per year).
steps.searchable_summary: Add a known quantity of polystyrene microspheres to processed pollen samples for calculating pollen influx.

steps.description: Pollen samples were counted using a Zeiss Axioimager microscope at ×400 and ×630 magnifications.
steps.searchable_summary: Count pollen grains and microspheres in spiked samples using a microscope.
outputs: Raw counts of pollen grains and microspheres per sample

steps.description: Pollen grains were identified using the Neotropical Pollen Database (Bush and Weng 2007), the Florida Institute of Technology pollen reference collection, and published pollen keys 33,[70][71][72] .
steps.searchable_summary: Identify counted pollen grains to taxonomic level using reference databases and keys.
inputs: Florida Institute of Technology pollen reference collection
outputs: Identified pollen taxa and counts per sample

steps.description: Pollen grains were recorded until either 300 terrestrial pollen grains were identified or 2000 microspheres were counted.For samples that contained >200 Poaceae grains, pollen identification continued until at least 100 non-Poaceae grains were identified or 1000 Poaceae grains were recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply specific counting criteria to determine when to stop counting for each sample.
inputs: Counting criteria (300 terrestrial grains, 2000 microspheres, Poaceae-specific rules)
outputs: Final pollen counts per sample meeting criteria

steps.description: Alnus is a pioneer tree of disturbed forest edges well known to be massively overrepresented in glacial forelands through being blown upslope and we exclude it from our Andean forest subtotals 33 .
steps.searchable_summary: Exclude Alnus pollen counts from specific ecological subtotals (e.g., Andean forest) to correct for overrepresentation bias.
inputs: Definition of ecological subtotals (e.g., Andean forest)

methods.description: Preparation and analysis of charcoal fragments in sediment samples, including organic matter dissolution, filtering, microscopic evaluation, and surface area calculation.

steps.description: Additional sediment samples (n = 705; volume = 0.5 cm 3 ) were placed in 3% H 2 O 2 for 24 h to dissolve organic matter for charcoal analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Treat sediment samples with hydrogen peroxide to dissolve organic matter.

steps.description: Samples were then filtered through a 180 μm mesh.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter treated sediment samples through a mesh to isolate charcoal fragments.

steps.description: The resulting residue was placed in a petri dish of water and evaluated for charcoal using an Olympus stereoscope at ×20 and ×32 magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: Observe filtered residue under a stereoscope to evaluate for the presence and characteristics of charcoal.

steps.description: Surface area (mm 2 /cm 3 ) of charcoal fragments were calculated using ImageJ software 76 .
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the surface area of charcoal fragments using image analysis software.

methods.description: Application of Detrended Correspondence Analysis (DCA) to fossil pollen data to analyze ecological patterns.

steps.description: To reduce noise in the analysis, only pollen types represented in 5 or more samples or occurring at least 2% of the pollen sum were included following 75 .
steps.searchable_summary: Filter pollen data to include only taxa meeting minimum frequency or abundance thresholds.
inputs: Identified pollen taxa counts per sample
Filtering criteria (>= 5 samples or >= 2% of pollen sum)

steps.description: The fossil pollen data were analyzed using Detrended Correspondence Analysis (DCA) in the vegan 73 package for R 74 .
steps.searchable_summary: Perform Detrended Correspondence Analysis (DCA) on the filtered pollen dataset using the vegan package in R.
outputs: DCA results (sample scores, species scores, eigenvalues, etc.)

methods.description: Calculation of various paleoclimate indices (Glacial Index, Organic Matter, CO3) and comparison of ecological trajectories across interglacial periods using normalized DCA scores.

steps.description: We take 11.8 ka to be the onset of the Holocene, but for all other interglacials we follow the temporal definitions of interglacial marine isotope stages (MIS) of 77 : MIS 1 11.8-0 ka, MIS 5 130-115 ka, MIS 7 243-191 ka, MIS 9 337-300 ka, MIS 11 424-374 ka, MIST 13 533-478 ka, and MIS 15 621-563 ka.
steps.searchable_summary: Define the temporal boundaries for specific Marine Isotope Stages (MIS) based on published chronologies.

steps.description: For the comparison of ecological trajectories during each interglacial, we define the onset of the interglacial functionally as the time that pollen influx (grains per cm 2 per yr) rose to five times the background amount of the preceding late glacial.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine the functional onset time for each interglacial period based on a threshold increase in pollen influx relative to background levels.
inputs: Pollen influx data (grains per cm2 per yr)
outputs: Defined onset time for each interglacial

steps.description: The DCA Axis 1 score for the sample at the onset of the interglacial was set to zero and the trajectory of sample scores progressing into the glacial were plotted relative to that baseline.
steps.searchable_summary: Normalize DCA Axis 1 scores for each interglacial by setting the score at the defined onset time to zero.
inputs: DCA Axis 1 scores for each sample
outputs: Normalized DCA Axis 1 scores relative to interglacial onset

steps.description: The DCA Axis 1 score for the sample at the onset of the interglacial was set to zero and the trajectory of sample scores progressing into the glacial were plotted relative to that baseline.
steps.searchable_summary: Plot the trajectory of normalized DCA Axis 1 scores over time for each interglacial period.
outputs: Plots of ecological trajectories over time

steps.description: Calculation of the glacial Index 26 is a Z-score of log-transformed sedimentary magnetic susceptibility and Ti/Ca data derived from X-ray fluorescence, at 250-year timesteps.The GI is the average z-score for both data sets.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate the Glacial Index (GI) by averaging the Z-scores of log-transformed magnetic susceptibility and Ti/Ca data at specified time intervals.

steps.description: Organic matter and CO 3 were calculated based on coulometry of CO 2 released when samples were combusted at 1000 °C, for details see supporting on-line materials of 26 .
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate organic matter and carbonate (CO3) content using coulometry of CO2 released during sample combustion.
